sources.notes: Statement and constraints: count pairs i<=j whose lcm is a product of exactly two primes; total n <= 2e5.
Official editorial and code checked for the exhaustive three-case classification and frequency formulas.
Cross-checked prime/semiprime classification, factor-incidence counting, and prime-square handling.
Second cross-check for the lcm exponent argument and cases p, p^2, and p*q.
Re-derived the frequency formula and randomized it against direct i<=j lcm enumeration on small arrays.

reference_candidates.notes: Luogu solution index; concrete consulted articles are listed in sources.

annotation.summary: Count pairs of indices i<=j whose values have an LCM equal to a product of exactly two primes, allowing equal primes.
annotation.constraints: n and total n are at most 2e5, and values are at most n, enabling a global smallest-prime-factor sieve and linear frequency arrays.
annotation.core_idea: Since each value divides the lcm, any usable value has at most two prime factors counted with multiplicity. Valid pairs are exactly: two distinct primes p,q; a semiprime s=pq paired with one of its prime factors (only once when p=q); or two occurrences of the same semiprime, including i=j. With frequency c[x] and P prime-valued occurrences, unequal-prime pairs contribute (P^2-sum c[p]^2)/2. Each semiprime s=pq contributes c[s](c[s]+1)/2 plus c[s](c[p]+(p!=q ? c[q] : 0)). A sieve precomputes primality and the two factors of every semiprime.
annotation.complexity: O(M log log M + sum n) time and O(M) memory for M=200000.
tricks: discard values with more than two prime factors
classify pairs into unequal primes, semiprime-with-factor, and equal semiprimes

solution_variants.name: offline-frequency-case-sum
solution_variants.summary: Preclassify values, aggregate unequal-prime pairs, then iterate semiprimes and add equal-semiprime and prime-factor partners.
solution_variants.complexity: O(M log log M) preprocessing and O(n) per test.

solution_variants.name: online-second-endpoint-contributions
solution_variants.summary: Scan values and count each valid pair when its second endpoint arrives, maintaining prime and factor-incidence frequencies.
solution_variants.complexity: O(M log log M) preprocessing and O(n) per test.

tags.evidence: The pair classification follows from componentwise maxima of prime exponents in lcm(x,y).
Values and lcms are classified by total prime-factor multiplicity, and all values above two are irrelevant.
The algorithm recovers the concrete factors p and q of each semiprime to count compatible prime-valued partners.
tags.description: Recover concrete prime factors and exponents of input values, usually with a smallest-prime-factor sieve or trial division, when factor identities drive the algorithm; excludes tasks using only generic divisibility tests.
tags.created_reason: The existing prime-factor-count tag records omega/Omega-style counts but does not identify problems that must recover concrete prime factors for compatibility logic.

tags.evidence: Correctness requires proving the three pair types exhaustive, including the p=q square boundary.
Valid index pairs are counted from value frequencies with cross products and c[s](c[s]+1)/2.
The online variant adds exactly the new compatible pairs contributed by each arriving value.
